page: Amazon Web Services r6i.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The configuration provides 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, supported by 54 MB of L3 cache. Memory capacity totals 384.0 GB of DDR4 RAM operating at 3 GHz, yielding a ratio of 8.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates solid capabilities across standard workloads. The processor achieves a PassMark CPU score of 48391.39 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 15739.0, both falling into the average to strong categories. Cryptographic performance is notable, with a PassMark CPU encryption test score of 43366.49, which is in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show 2487.28 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 163.34 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. In database-related tests, the instance handles 45.38 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 21115.65 on PassMark database operations.

While the instance demonstrates strong performance in specific areas like image processing and small-model LLM inference, it exhibits limitations with larger models. For instance, LLM text generation using a 70B model drops to 2.68 tokens per second, placing it in the bottom 25% of benchmarked systems. Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and the lack of local storage or GPUs, this instance is suited for memory-intensive applications such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications that do not require local NVMe storage or heavy parallel GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services r6i.12xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8375C processor operating at 3.5 GHz, delivering 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores. The system features 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB. There is no local storage or GPU hardware included, and network connectivity is established at a baseline of 19 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show strong capabilities in encryption and image processing, alongside average database operation speeds of 21115.65 on PassMark. However, text generation for large 70B language models is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25% at 2.68 tokens per second. This instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and data processing tasks that benefit from high RAM density without requiring local SSD storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6i.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 platform featuring 384 GB of DDR4 RAM and 48 vCPUs for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz
